w:r.w:t: Your complete guide to AI-powered B2B outbound sales

Flowfiy is an AI-powered B2B outbound sales platform that automates your entire prospecting pipeline. You describe your business and ideal customer once — Flowfiy's five AI agents do everything else: find leads, research companies, score prospects, and write a personalized email sequence for every single one.
Manually building lead lists takes hours and still produces generic, unresearched contacts
Copy-pasted cold emails get ignored — buyers can tell when you haven't done your homework
Good SDRs are expensive and can only work so many hours a day
Most outreach tools automate sending — but not the thinking or the writing

Flowfiy doesn't just send emails faster. It thinks before it sends. Five Claude AI agents chain together to replicate what a great SDR would spend hours doing — understanding your ICP, researching each company, deciding if they're a real fit, and crafting a message that references their specific situation.
AI automatically discovers leads matching your exact ICP
Copy company info from websites by hand
AI scrapes and analyzes every company website automatically
Write the same email 50 times with minor tweaks
AI writes a unique, research-backed email for every lead
Guess which leads are worth your time
Pay $500+/month for tools with usage markups
Pay API providers at cost — no markup, ever
Founders doing early-stage outbound and want to move fast without hiring an SDR
SDRs and AEs who want to 10x their outreach volume without sacrificing quality
Growth teams running ABM campaigns who need deep personalization at scale
Sales agencies managing multiple clients who want one platform for all campaigns
Managed AI means Claude is fully managed by Flowfiy. You connect your own API accounts for Apollo, Apify, Gmail, and Calendly. This means:
You pay providers directly at their published rates — Flowfiy adds zero markup
Your data stays in your accounts, not ours
No artificial usage limits based on your plan tier
Start free: 50 lead generations at no cost, no credit card required

Every lead generation run passes through five AI agents that run in sequence. Each agent takes the output of the previous step and adds intelligence. Here's exactly what happens from the moment you click "Generate Leads":
Reads your business profile → builds search parameters
Searches Apollo's database → returns real people
Scores each lead 0–100 → decides QUALIFIED or DISQUALIFIED
Writes subject line + 3-touch email sequence per qualified lead
Reads your business profile, service offering, ICP description, and target parameters. Asks Claude to refine your ideal customer profile into concrete Apollo search filters.
Job titles, industries, company sizes, geographies, qualification criteria
Calls Apollo.io with the filters from Agent 1. Retrieves real people: name, email, job title, company name, company website, LinkedIn URL. You set how many leads to find.
Raw lead records (up to your requested count)
For each lead, scrapes the company's website using Apify. Analyzes business model, tech stack, growth signals, hiring trends, and company size. Compares to your ICP.
Scores each lead 0–100 based on how closely they match your ICP criteria. Identifies the best opportunity angle and personalization hooks specific to this company.
Only runs for QUALIFIED leads. Uses everything learned in steps 1–4 to write a subject line, email body, and two follow-ups — referencing real details about the company.
Subject line, Email body, Follow-up 1, Follow-up 2
Agents 3–5 run in parallel batches of 5 leads at a time. A list of 20 leads typically completes in 2–5 minutes. The more detail you give in your business profile, the better every agent performs.
Follow these five steps in order. The whole setup takes about 10 minutes.
Go to flowfiy.com and click Get Started Free
Enter your email and create a password, or sign in with Google
Verify your email — you'll receive a confirmation link
After verification, you're redirected to the onboarding wizard
Your account starts with 50 free lead generations — no credit card needed
Enter your organization or company name (e.g., "Acme Sales" or "John's Agency")
This creates your workspace — a private space for your leads, campaigns, and settings
You can invite team members later from the Settings page
Set Up Your Business Profile & ICP
This is the most important step. The quality of everything Claude generates depends on how clearly you describe your business and ideal customer. Take 5 minutes here — it pays off on every lead.
CRM software for B2B SaaS sales teams
Your ideal customer in plain English
B2B SaaS companies with 50-500 employees, Series A or B funded, that have a sales team of 5+ reps
Sales reps spending too much time on data entry, low CRM adoption, poor pipeline visibility
The only CRM that auto-logs calls and emails, so reps spend 80% less time on admin
How you want emails to sound

The ICP Description field is the most powerful input. Include company stage (seed/Series A/B), team size, tech stack if relevant, and any buying signals that indicate good fit (recent fundraise, hiring SDRs, expanding into new markets).
Go to Settings → Integrations. You'll see five integration slots. Here's what each one does and where to get the key:
All 5 AI agents — the brain of the system
Lead search — finds people matching your ICP
Sends outreach from your own Gmail inbox
Adds your booking link to every email automatically

You can use Flowfiy without Apollo by importing your own lead list via CSV. You can use Flowfiy without Apify — company analysis will be less detailed but still works. You cannot generate outreach without a Claude AI (managed by Flowfiy).
Each integration card has a Validate button — click it after saving your key
A green checkmark means the connection is working
A red error means the key is wrong or the account has insufficient permissions
Common issue: Apollo's free plan has very limited API credits — upgrade to a paid plan for reliable lead discovery
Gmail requires you to re-authorize if you change your Google account password
Go to Leads in the left sidebar
Give your list a descriptive name — you'll thank yourself later (e.g., "SaaS CTOs — US — June 2026")
Set the number of leads — start with 15–20 for your first run to check quality
Click Generate — the pipeline starts running in the background
You'll see the list status update in real time as agents complete each stage:
Your job is in the queue, about to start
Agent 1 is analyzing your ICP and building Apollo search filters
Agent 2 is searching Apollo for matching leads
Agents 3+4 are scraping websites, analyzing companies, and scoring leads
All done! Your leads are ready to review
Something went wrong (usually a missing or invalid API key)
A run of 20 leads takes roughly 2–5 minutes. You don't need to stay on the page — come back when it's done. Large runs (100+ leads) can take 15–30 minutes.
Already have a list of prospects? Use Import CSV instead of Generate Leads.
Click Import CSV on the Leads page
Download the template to see the expected columns
Upload your file — Flowfiy creates the lead list and skips Apollo (Agent 2)
Agents 3, 4, and 5 still run on your imported leads — they get full research and personalization
Just discovered — not yet processed by agents
Agents are currently working on this lead
Passed ICP criteria — outreach copy is ready
Did not meet criteria — wrong size, industry, or weak fit
You have sent them an outreach email

Every qualified lead gets a score that reflects how closely the company matches your ICP. This is calculated by Agent 4 using your ICP description, target industries, company size preferences, and the intelligence gathered in Agent 3.
Prioritize these first, review copy carefully
Use your judgment — may still convert
Click any qualified lead to see the full outreach copy Agent 5 wrote. You'll find:
Subject Line — personalized to the lead's role and company context
Email Body — references specific company details from the website scrape (products, recent news, team size, tech stack)
Follow-Up 1 — a brief check-in for leads who don't reply (typically sent on day 3)
Follow-Up 2 — a final brief touchpoint (typically sent on day 7)
Calendly Link — automatically embedded if you connected Calendly
You can edit any field before sending. You can also click Regenerate to have Agent 5 write a fresh version.

The email body is not a template — Agent 5 writes each one fresh based on what Agent 3 found about that specific company. If a lead's website had limited information (common for small companies), the copy will be more generic. Apify scraping helps significantly here.
Go to Campaigns in the left sidebar → click New Campaign
Give it a name (e.g., "SaaS CTOs — June Outreach")
Select the lead list you want to draw from
Choose your Gmail sending address (the email account you connected)
Paste your Calendly link if you want it appended to every email
Set a daily send limit — the default is 50 per day, which respects Gmail's sending limits
Click Save to create the campaign in DRAFT status
Before you activate the campaign, you can review — and optionally edit — the outreach copy for each lead. This is your last chance to personalize further or skip leads that aren't right.
Click any lead in the campaign to view or edit their email copy
Toggle a lead to Skip if you don't want to contact them
Make any edits you want — they're saved automatically
Click Activate Campaign to start sending
Emails go out at a paced rate respecting the 50/hour limit
Each email comes from your own Gmail inbox — not a shared platform IP
Each email is addressed personally and references company-specific research
You can pause the campaign at any time — emails already in the queue are cancelled
Sending — emails going out per your daily limit
Paused by you — resume any time
All leads in the campaign have been contacted
The campaign page shows real-time metrics:
Sent — total emails sent so far
Replies — leads who replied (tracked via Gmail thread ID)
Meetings — leads who booked a meeting via your Calendly link
Per-lead status — each lead shows SENT → OPENED → REPLIED → MEETING_BOOKED
Most replies come on Follow-Up 2, not the first email. Be patient and let the sequence run all 3 touches before judging results.
Be specific in your ICP description.
Be specific in your ICP description.
"B2B SaaS with 50–500 employees, Series A/B funded, with a sales team" beats "software companies". Specificity makes every agent more accurate.
Run your first lead list with 15–20 leads before scaling to 100+. Check that the companies and titles match what you expected. Tune your ICP if not.
A clear call-to-action with a direct booking link dramatically increases conversion from reply to meeting. Agent 5 will embed it naturally into the copy.
Connect Apify — even the free tier.
Connect Apify — even the free tier.
Website scraping dramatically improves personalization. Without it, Agent 3 has less to work with and the outreach copy will be more generic.
Leads scoring above 70 are your highest-priority targets. Start campaigns with these before moving to lower-scored leads.
AI gets you 90% of the way there. A 2-minute human review catches the occasional awkward line and makes the email sound authentically you.
Treat disqualified leads as ICP feedback.
Treat disqualified leads as ICP feedback.
If too many leads are getting disqualified, your ICP might be too narrow or your description unclear. Open a few disqualified leads to see Agent 4's reasoning.
'Professional' can sound stiff and formal in cold email. Conversational is warmer and gets better response rates for initial outreach.
Don't change your business profile mid-campaign.
Don't change your business profile mid-campaign.
Changing your ICP or business profile will affect future lead generation but not leads already in existing lists. Changing it mid-campaign creates inconsistency.
Data consistently shows that follow-up 2 (the final touch) generates a significant portion of all replies. Don't give up after the first email.

A generation is one lead going through the full 5-agent pipeline — researched, qualified, and with outreach copy written. Disqualified leads still count as a generation (research was done). Importing and skipping leads before generation does not count.
Trying Flowfiy — no time limit, no credit card

You can upgrade or downgrade your plan at any time from the Billing page. Annual billing is available at a discount. All plans include all features — plans differ only in generation count and team seats.

One lead going through the full pipeline — researched by Agent 3, scored by Agent 4, and with outreach copy written by Agent 5. Leads that are disqualified still count (research was done). Importing leads or skipping them before running the pipeline does not count.
Why do I need my own API keys?

Managed AI means you pay Anthropic, Apollo, and Apify directly at their published rates — Flowfiy adds no markup. Your API usage, data, and rate limits all stay in your own accounts. You're not sharing credits with other users.
How accurate is the qualification score?

The score reflects how closely a company matches your specific ICP criteria. Leads scoring 70+ have significantly higher response rates in practice. If scores seem off, the most common cause is a vague ICP description — try making it more specific.
Can I use Flowfiy without Apollo?

Yes. Use the CSV Import feature to upload your own lead list. Flowfiy will still run company analysis (Agent 3), qualification (Agent 4), and personalized outreach generation (Agent 5) on your imported leads. You skip only the Apollo discovery step.
Can I use Flowfiy without Apify?

Yes. Company analysis will be less detailed since there's no website content to scrape. The pipeline still runs, but personalization hooks will be based on publicly available information rather than the company's own website copy.
Is my data and are my API keys secure?

All API keys are encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM encryption before being stored. Your Gmail access token is used only to send emails on your behalf — we never read your inbox. Emails send from your own Gmail account, not from Flowfiy's servers.
Can I edit the AI-generated emails?
Yes — you can edit the subject line, email body, and both follow-ups for any lead before sending. You can also click Regenerate to have Agent 5 write a completely fresh version.
What happens to leads that are disqualified?

Disqualified leads stay in your list with their score and Agent 4's reasoning. You can review them, manually override the disqualification, and generate outreach copy for any lead you decide is worth contacting.
How long does lead generation take?
About 2–5 minutes for 20 leads. The pipeline processes leads in batches of 5 simultaneously. Larger runs (50 leads) take roughly 10–15 minutes. You don't need to stay on the page while it runs.
Why are some companies showing less personalized emails?

If a company has a minimal website (one-page sites, under-construction pages, or sites that block scrapers), Agent 3 has less to work with. Connecting Apify and ensuring the company_website field is populated in your lead list helps significantly.

Each account belongs to one organization (workspace). Agencies managing multiple clients should use team member invitations to collaborate in one workspace, or create separate accounts per client. Multi-org support is on the roadmap.
